seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total build cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: composite: skin as forest-fire watchtower surveillance and generalize the seed from a grid to an arbitrary weighted forest-cell graph with a per-cell build cost and a hop-radius R (R-dominating set), turning uniform k-center into a genuinely weighted min-cost R-covering where cost-aware and coverage-aware heuristics diverge; the all-towers construction is the calibrated baseline B.
title: Firewatch: Watchtower Placement on a Forest Graph
strategies: build a tower on every cell (reproduces the checker baseline B, ratio = 0.1)
coverage-greedy: repeatedly build on the cell that newly covers the most cells (ignores cost)
cost-ratio greedy: repeatedly build on the cell maximizing newly-covered / build-cost
cost-ratio greedy followed by redundancy pruning (drop towers whose coverage is fully redundant, most-expensive first)
local-search swaps / LP-rounding of the fractional covering relaxation
